ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Bifurcated Issue Of Fees
Off Calendar Feb-08-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Trisha Friedeberg for and with Barbara Simon (Trustee).

Ms. Friedeberg stated that all litigation is over, that there is no objection, that this is a nine and a half year Trust administration, that the Trust was not under Court supervision so there was no need to file accountings and that the petition is part of the settlement agreement. The Petition for Approval of Fees and Costs to Trustee and Attorneys is taken under submission.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: D. Duong. Not reported.

Off Calendar Sep-28-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Trisha Friedeberg for (trustee) Barbara Simon.

The Court stated that it may have to bifurcate out the fee issues. Ms. Friedeberg stated that she understood and that there is no argument regarding the non fee issues. The Court took the Accounting Petition under submission with the exception of the fee issues which were bifurcated and continued to November 14, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Aug-15-2016 Continued To Sep-19-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Bruce Feder for petitioner Barbara Simon.

The Court stated his view that an amendment of the petition would be necessary, in order to give notice to everyone of the modification to the original petition. Mr. Feder agreed to file an amendment in compliance with the Court's direction. The matter was continued to September 19, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for a status hearing on the filing of the amendment, and for the petition as amended should the amendment be timely filed and served prior to that hearing date.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Chan. (Not reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Mar-24-2015 Continued To Apr-21-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Bruce Feder for Barbara Simon. Daniel Newbold for Johanna Resnick.

The court stated that objections to the accounting as well as a settlement agreement have been filed. Mr. Newbold reported that they have resolved and the beneficiaries will be withdrawing the objections and that counsel for 1 of the parties had requested a continuance to be able to file the withdrawals. Mr. Feder stated that he and Mr. Simron are in agreement. Mr. Newbold stated that he believed Mr. Simron was not in attendance because his client no longer has an interest, that he would like a court order approving the accounting, that the procedural issues have been addressed in his declaration, that notice has been given to Mr. Feder's office, that he can re-file the proof of service and that he will respond to the examiner's notes regarding the way title is held on the bank statements. The accounting petition was continued to April 21, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Mar-10-2015 Continued To Mar-24-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Feb-03-2015 Continued To Mar-10-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Bruce Feder for (trustee) Barbara Simon. Daniel Newbold for (petitioner) Joanna Resnick. Steve Simrin for (objector) Nick Stevens. Francesca Callejo for (objector/beneficiary) Norman Ayers.

Mr. Newbold stated that they have resolved all issues and are in the process of formatting a settlement agreement, that he will submit the original bank statements shortly, that he has provided courtesy copies and has filed a declaration in response to the examiner's notes, and requested a 30 day continuance. The accounting petition was continued to March 10, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 with the expectation that papers reflecting resolution of the matters will be filed.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Santiago. (Not Reported)

Petition To Compel Johanna Resnick To Comply With The Terms Of Settlement Agreement Dated January 10, 2013; To Compel Distributions From The Herbert Resnick Trust; To Compel An Accounting; To Correct Payments To Nic Stevens And Norman Ayers; To Determine Construction Of Trust And Instruct Trustee; To Compel Distributions To Beneficiaries Of The Elizabeth Resnick Revocable Trust; And For Johanna Re
Off Calendar Aug-04-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Trisha Friedeberg for and with Barbara Simon. Daniel Newbold for Johanna Resnick (respondent). Steve Simrin for Nicole Stevens (beneficiary). Francesca Callejo for Norman Ayers (beneficiary).

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to venue (San Francisco vs. Alameda County), the need for Ms. Resnick to provide an accounting, distribution of the funds to be used for educational purposes, the issue of whether interest should be paid on said distributions, whether the settlement agreement included a purchase price of the property, checks for the RBC account issued for $22,000.00 less than what should have been, attorneys fees, etc. The court ordered an accounting on the RBC account from December 31, 2011. Status on filing of the accounting is set for September 15, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The petition to compel Johanna Resnick to comply with the terms of settlement agreement, etc. was taken under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

Petition For Order Confirming Certain Assets Are Trust Property
Off Calendar Mar-04-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Bruce Feder and Trisha Friedeberg for and with Barbara Simon (Trustee). Daniel Newbold for Johanna Resnick (Beneficiary). Steven Simrin for Nick Steven (Beneficiary).

The Court's concerns with regard to IRAs not in the Trust, three different Schedule As, one handwritten all dated the same date were addressed. Mr. Feder stated that the settlement agreeement is to distribute the income and principal and that he explained the reason for the different IRAs, that some were the Trustor's own and some she had inherited from her father. Mr. Newbold stated that the settlement agreement in place is to divide it three ways. The Court instruced Mr. Feder to file a further declaration regarding the IRAs and the matter was taken under submission pending receipt of Mr. Feder's declartion.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy Abasse.
